title: Jonah 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Jonah
paragraphs: 1 The LORD spoke to Jonah son of Amittai. He said,
2 “Get up! Go to the great city of Nineveh. Speak against it, because its evil has come up to Me.”

3 But Jonah got up to run away to Tarshish, away from the LORD. He went down to Joppa and found a ship going to Tarshish. He paid the price and went on board. He wanted to sail to Tarshish, away from the LORD.
4 Then the LORD sent a strong wind on the sea. A very strong storm came, and the ship almost broke apart.

5 The sailors were afraid. Each one cried out to his own god. They threw the ship’s goods into the sea to make it lighter. But Jonah had gone down inside the ship. He lay down there and fell into a deep sleep.
6 The captain came to him and said, “How can you sleep? Get up and call to your God! Maybe this God will think of us, so we will not die.”
7 The sailors said to each other, “Come! Let us throw lots to find who caused this trouble.” So they threw lots, and the lot fell on Jonah.
8 They said, “Tell us now. Who caused this trouble for us? What is your job? Where do you come from? What is your country, and who are your people?”
9 Jonah answered, “I am a Hebrew. I worship the LORD, the God of heaven. He made the sea and the dry land.”
10 Then the men were even more afraid. They said to him, “What have you done?” They knew he was running from the LORD, because he had told them.
11 The sea was getting worse and worse. So they said to Jonah, “What should we do to you to calm the sea?”
12 He answered, “Pick me up and throw me into the sea. Then it will become calm for you. I know this big storm is my fault.”
13 But the men rowed hard to get back to dry land. They could not, because the sea was getting wilder against them.
14 So they cried out to the LORD, “Please, O LORD, do not let us die for this man’s life. Do not blame us for killing an innocent man. You, O LORD, have done what You wanted.”
15 Then they picked Jonah up and threw him into the sea. The wild sea became calm.
16 Then the men greatly feared the LORD. They offered a sacrifice to the LORD and made vows to Him.
17 Now the LORD sent a great fish to swallow Jonah. Jonah was inside the fish for three days and three nights.

title: Jonah 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Jonah
paragraphs: 1 From inside the fish, Jonah prayed to the LORD his God.
2 He said: "In my trouble I called to the LORD, and He answered me. From the belly of Sheol I called for help, and You heard my voice.
3 You threw me into the deep, into the heart of the sea. The current spun around me. All Your waves and high waves rolled over me.
4 Then I said, 'I have been sent away from Your sight. Yet I will look once more toward Your holy temple.'
5 The waters covered me to take my life. The deep waters closed around me. The seaweed wrapped around my head.
6 I went down to the roots of the mountains. The earth shut me in forever! But You brought my life up from the pit, O LORD my God!
7 When my life was slipping away, I remembered the LORD. My prayer went up to You, to Your holy temple.
8 Those who hold on to worthless idols turn away from His steady love.
9 But I, with songs of thanks, will offer a sacrifice to You. I will do what I have promised. Salvation comes from the LORD!"
10 Then the LORD commanded the fish, and it spit Jonah out onto dry land.

title: Jonah 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Jonah
paragraphs: 1 Then the word of the LORD came to Jonah a second time.
2 "Get up! Go to the great city of Nineveh. Preach the message I give you."
3 This time Jonah got up and went to Nineveh, as the LORD had told him. Nineveh was a very large city. It took three days to cross it.
4 On his first day, Jonah walked into the city. He preached, "In forty more days Nineveh will be destroyed!"
5 The people of Nineveh believed God. They called for a fast and put on sackcloth. Everyone did this, from the most important to the least.
6 When the news reached the king of Nineveh, he got up from his royal seat. He took off his royal coat. He covered himself with sackcloth and sat in ashes.
7 Then he sent out this order in Nineveh: "By the command of the king and his leaders: No person or animal, no cattle or sheep, may taste anything. They must not eat or drink.
8 Let both people and animals be covered with sackcloth. Let everyone call out to God with all their heart. Each one must turn from his evil ways and from the violence in his hands.
9 Who knows? God may turn and change His mind. He may turn from His strong anger, so we will not perish."
10 God saw what they did. They turned from their evil ways. So He changed His mind about the trouble He had said He would send.

title: Jonah 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Jonah
paragraphs: 1 But Jonah was very upset, and he became angry.

2 So he prayed to the LORD, “O LORD, is this not what I said back home? 2b This is why I quickly tried to run away to Tarshish. 2c I knew that You are a gracious and kind God, slow to get angry. 2d You are full of steady love, and You hold back from sending trouble.
3 “Now, O LORD, please take my life. It is better for me to die than to live.”
4 But the LORD replied, “Do you have any right to be angry?”
5 Then Jonah left the city and sat down east of it. 5b He built himself a small shelter and sat in its shade. 5c He waited to see what would happen to the city.
6 Then the LORD God sent a plant, and it grew up over Jonah’s head. 6b It gave him shade to help him feel better, and Jonah was very glad about the plant.
7 When dawn came the next day, God sent a worm. 7b The worm attacked the plant, and it dried up.
8 As the sun was rising, God sent a very hot east wind. 8b The sun beat down on Jonah’s head, and he felt very weak. 8c He wished to die, saying, “It is better for me to die than to live.”
9 Then God asked Jonah, “Do you have any right to be angry about the plant?” 9b “I do,” he replied. “I am angry enough to die!”
10 But the LORD said, “You cared about the plant. 10b You did not care for it or grow it. 10c It came up in a night and died in a night.
11 “Then should I not care about the great city of Nineveh? 11b It has more than 120,000 people who do not know right from left, and many cattle too.”
